summary: FPV drone operators from the 4th OMSBr destroyed a camouflaged Ukrainian T-64 tank in Konstantinovka, while Ukrainian FPV drones damaged Russian Gerbera drone launchers in Crimea. Russian forces struck Ukrainian UAV control points in Kruzhok, Sumy region, and used FAB-3000 bombs with UMPK on VDV positions in Konstantinovka. Cluster RBC-500 bombs with UMPC were deployed against Ukrainian UAV launch sites near Maryevka, Dnipropetrovsk region, and FAB-500 bombs with UMPC hit airfields in Shevchenko, Donetsk People's Republic. A mobile fire group in a Russian border region intercepted a Ukrainian strike drone at over 1.5 km using the "Yolka" anti-drone system, causing its charge to detonate. Ukrainian forces survived an FPV drone attack in the Kostiantinivka direction, with footage showing a drone detector in use. Russian Ka-52 crew was eliminated near Nadiivka after a crash landing, with additional drones striking during evacuation. Ukrainian EW interference was demonstrated with a 15-second FPV drone landing command. The "Рассвет" satellite network, aiming to rival Starlink, launched its first 16 satellites on March 23, with plans for 250 by late 2027 and 730 by 2030. A ring-shaped wing drone design, previously debated, is now being mass-produced by "Ushkuynik" in FPV format.
